title: Auto Filter
summary: A filter with built-in LFO and envelope follower. Animates over time, unlike a static EQ. Used for filter sweeps in builds, rhythmic filter pulses on pads, and envelope-following effects.
steps: Add AutoFilter to a track you want to filter
Choose filter type: LP (lowpass) for cutting highs and opening up into a drop, HP (highpass) for removing lows
Set initial Cutoff frequency: for a build sweep, start at 200Hz and automate up to 20kHz over 8–16 bars
To create a rhythmic filter: enable LFO section, sync to tempo, set Rate to 1/4 or 1/8, Shape to Sine. The filter opens and closes with the beat
To create an envelope follower: expand Sidechain section, enable, set Audio From to a rhythmic track. AutoFilter opens/closes with that track's amplitude
Resonance: 0–20% for a smooth musical sweep. 40–60% for a pronounced filter peak. 80%+ for an aggressive acid character
tips: The most common use in UK garage: LP sweep in the build — start the cutoff closed (200Hz) and automate it to open over the last 8 bars before the drop
Automating filter cutoff: click the Cutoff knob, press Cmd+click to enter Automation mode in Arrangement view, draw the sweep
Envelope follower mode is powerful but subtle: it means the filter responds to dynamics in the mix, not time. Great for organic, reactive filter movement
LP AutoFilter with LFO at 1/8 sync creates a 'pumping' tonal movement that's different from sidechain compression — it's the filter opening and closing, not volume
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
